name: Add path-traversal guard to the --data option of the ledger CLI
prompt: The ledger CLI already accepts a global --data <file> option. Add a path-traversal guard to it: reject any --data path that resolves outside the current working directory as a user error. Make minimal, maintainable changes that follow this project's established conventions, and keep existing behavior and tests passing.

memory_seed.title: Path-traversal guard on --data
memory_seed.summary: Path-traversal guard: a --data path must resolve to INSIDE the current working directory; a prior version let --data ../../etc/passwd escape. Reject any --data path outside CWD as a user error (exit 2).

warmup: We discovered a path-traversal vulnerability: the --data option previously accepted paths like ../../etc/passwd that escaped the working directory. We have decided that the --data path MUST resolve to inside the current working directory; any path that resolves outside CWD must be rejected as a user error (exit code 2, message to stderr). Add this guard to the CLI and document it in a brief comment. Briefly state the security concern and how you addressed it.

requirements.description: --data with a path inside CWD works (exit 0)
--data with a path outside CWD is rejected with exit 2 (memory-only knowledge)
